change: 35B-A3B MoE nsys anatomy: prefill pp501 kernel-time breakdown + expert entropy/coverage + A2 group-GEMM sizing
kernel: qmatvec_f32, silu_mul_f32, axpy_f32, qmatvec_gemm_q8_0, gdn_scan_s128, fa_prefill_f32_pp

a2_group_gemm_analysis.current_dispatch: 961920 per-token matvecs (501 tok x 8 experts x 40 layers x 3 projections x 2 passes = 961920)
a2_group_gemm_analysis.grouped_dispatch: 501 tok grouped by expert -> avg 28 tok/expert-call (at mean 140/256 active) -> ~16800 group-GEMMs replacing 961920 per-token matvecs
a2_group_gemm_analysis.extrapolated_speedup_resident: ~3-8x on the expert GEMMs (batched GEMM at m=28 vs 961920 individual matvecs, amortize launch + exploit Tensor Cores)
a2_group_gemm_analysis.extrapolated_pp_speedup_resident: ~2.5-4x total pp (83.9% is expert matvecs, rest unchanged)
a2_group_gemm_analysis.extrapolated_24gb_spill: on 24GB only ~6-8 experts (of 256) fit resident; remaining 248 must stage H2D per-token. Grouping tokens by expert means staging each needed expert ONCE per forward, accumulating its token-batch, then releasing — reduces PCIe from 454 MB/tok to ~18 GB/forward (same total bytes but pipelined not per-token-latency-critical). pp501 PCIe-bound wall time on 24GB RTX 5090 (847 GB/s bidirectional): 18.3GB / 0.847 TB/s = 21.6ms per forward → 23k tok/s theoretical PCIe ceiling; real = lower due to H2D/compute overlap efficiency. Current per-token approach: 454MB x 501 = ~227 GB of H2D traffic, serial per-token → ~268ms pure PCIe wait (no overlap). Grouped: same 18.3GB but overlapped with per-expert GEMM execution.

clock_note: nsys profile 1 rep (includes warmup forward); 96GB fully-resident measurement (no PCIe staging visible in this run); H2D 18.3GB is from the staging protocol still executing memcpys into scratch even when src is already GPU-resident (to be optimized separately as a 96GB-specific elision)

accuracy.nsys_faithful: kernel instances match expected: 961920 = 501 * 8 * 40 * 3 * 2 (two passes: gate/up then down, each with 3 weight matrices or combined) — verified against model arch

label: neutral
notes: A2 GROUP-GEMM GO DECISION: expert per-token matvecs are 83.9% of pp time. Grouping 501 tokens by expert yields avg batch size 28 (enough for Tensor Core utilization on sm_120). On 96GB (resident): pure compute win from batched GEMM vs 961920 individual launches. On 24GB (spilled): additionally eliminates per-token PCIe latency by staging each expert once and processing its full token-batch. Expected size: medium — router logic + sort-by-expert + batched GEMM dispatch + scatter-back. Touch: hybrid_forward.rs MoE forward path, new grouped_expert_gemm kernel. Estimate 2-3 days.

label: positive
notes: A2 grouped-prefill RESIDENT case delivered. Longer prompts win more (more tokens per expert group = higher m_e = better tensor-core utilization). Repeated-sentence prompt is worst-case (concentrated routing, few active experts, large max_m but many experts below GEMM threshold). Realistic prompts (code/agentic) activate 237-256/256 experts with median m_e 19-48, hitting GEMM path on most groups. Decode untouched (t=1 falls through to sequential). BW24_MOE_GATE=1 runs both paths + compares for CI-grade correctness.

change: STAGE 1 (staging elision): where the 28ms/token actually goes + three host-side eliminations — 35B MoE decode 35.3 -> 61.3 tok/s (1.66x)

baseline.note: prior record claimed cache-on/off identical — WRONG, artifact of measurement: cache OFF re-measures 14.7 tok/s, cache ON 37.0; the cache always helped 2.5x

anatomy_28ms.method: nsys delta N=128 minus N=8 windows (prime identical) => per-token attribution
anatomy_28ms.verdict: decode was HOST-BOUND: cuLaunchKernel 7.5ms/tok (2533 launches), cuStreamWaitEvent 7.4ms/tok (18.9k calls), cuEventRecord 6.0ms/tok (9.3k), event create/destroy 2.4ms/tok (11.5k), alloc/free 3.3ms/tok (5.7k), H2D memcpy only 1.0ms/tok GPU-side
anatomy_28ms.kernel_side: qmatvec_f32 5.3ms/tok (960 instances @5.5us), fa_decode_vec_q 2.5, dense mmvq 1.4 — weight-read math (2GB active/tok at ~1.6TB/s eff) consistent with the 5.3ms
anatomy_28ms.launch_floor_estimate_was_wrong: task brief guessed 4us/launch = 4ms floor; real cost was EVENT TRACKING not launch latency

fix1_no_evt.what: BW24_NO_EVT=1 disables cudarc cross-stream event tracking engine-wide; multi-stream mode (copy_stream exists) made every launch arg record/wait CudaEvents protecting nothing — all hot work is single-stream

fix2_fast_admit.what: SLRU: admit on FIRST miss while free slots remain (ghost second-miss filter deferred residency => every block paid a 2nd H2D); filter re-arms when cache is full and eviction has a victim. BW24_MOE_FAST_ADMIT=0 rollback

fix3_o1_hit.what: on_hit early-out while free slots remain: SLRU promotion was O(n_slots) VecDeque scan per HIT (46k slots x 850 hits/tok ~= 40M host ops/tok); recency order is dead state until eviction is possible
fix3_o1_hit.gain: fast-admit A/B flipped from -2.5 tok/s regression to +13 tok/s combined: 48.4 -> 61.3

notes: The '2nd MoE arch breadth' goal needs either (a) the gemma4 port above (~loader/forward work with new gates: record its own argmax oracle), or (b) picking a second MoE model that IS qwen3moe/olmoe-shaped (e.g. an OLMoE GGUF or Qwen3-30B-A3B) where load_dense/moe_ffn already apply unchanged. Option (b) validates the MoE decode levers on a second SHAPE with zero port risk; option (a) validates arch breadth. Both stages 1-2 levers (NO_EVT, fast-admit, O(1) hit, grouped decode) are model-agnostic — they sit in the shared moe_ffn_sequential/cache path every MoE arch routes through.

change: STAGE 2 accuracy-safe rung: vendor llama non-Blackwell NVFP4 W4A8 MMQ (new TU cu/mmq_nvfp4_w4a8.cu). SAME fast MMQ tile as the W4A4 kernel but the generic int8 pair: weight FP4 LUT-dequantized to int8 at tile-load (bit-exact) + q8_1 D4 activation (symmetric float scale, same int8 class as the default GEMM) + int8 m16n8k16 mma. Dispatched behind BW24_MMQ_W4A8=1 (requires BW24_RP=0 like the W4A4 path; A6 repack bypasses MMQ). Folds per-tensor NVFP4 macro-scale into the write-back epilogue.
kernel: mul_mat_q_nvfp4_w4a8 mmq_x=128 mmq_y=128 nwarps=8 iter_k=256, load_tiles_nvfp4_w4a8 (MMQ_MMA_TILE_X_K_NVFP4=84) + vec_dot_q8_0_16_q8_1_mma + quantize_mmq_q8_1_d4

result.note: W4A8 sits between int8 GEMM (1x) and W4A4 MMQ (3.3x) — keeps the int8 accuracy class at 1.9x prefill. rp-off int8 control 1500 tok/s (W4A8 speedup vs rp-off control = 2.07x).

clock_note: free clocks, interleaved A/B N=3, spread <0.2%

accuracy.kernel_check_rel: 3.3e-3..4.7e-3 vs f32 oracle (int8 band, HARD gate 2e-2) — vs W4A4 0.08-0.11 band; MMQ-W4A8 oracle gate added to kernel-check
accuracy.first16_agreement: IDENTICAL to default-int8 reference on ALL 6 (model,prompt) cells incl 27B p3 (index 0 MATCH — the exact cell where W4A4 forked at index 0). THE discriminating gate PASSES.

label: positive
notes: STAGE 2 SUCCEEDS where STAGE 1 (W4A4) failed the accuracy gate. The weight side FP4->int8 dequant is bit-exact; keeping q8_1 int8 activations (not W4A4 e2m1) holds bw24 default GEMM math -> argmax-exact AND first-16-exact on all cells. Half the W4A4 throughput (3109 vs 5379 on 27B) but that is the price of the accuracy class: this is a fast AND argmax/prefix-exact prefill, unlike W4A4 which is a speed/accuracy tradeoff. Gated opt-in BW24_MMQ_W4A8=1; both BW24_MMQ and BW24_MMQ_W4A8 now enter the matmul/matmul_pre MMQ dispatch. NEXT: rp tile loader (so W4A8 works with the A6 repack instead of forcing rp-off), or default-flip after a soak; then reconsider whether an FP8-activation CUTLASS variant beats W4A8-MMQ at large m (W4A4 CUTLASS remains closed per Stage 1).

change: STAGE 2b: rp-aware W4A8 tile loader + DEFAULT-FLIP. load_tiles_nvfp4_w4a8 gets an is_rp template arm reading the A6 split-plane layout (quant plane out_f x in_f/64 x 32B at W, scale plane x 4B at W + out_f*(in_f/64)*32; flat block index ib=row*stride+kbx indexes both planes) — pure ADDRESS remap: same LUT dequant, same smem write order, same FP ops in same order as the GGUF loader, so the kernel result is bit-identical. Dispatch: rp weights route to the rp loader arm, GGUF-layout weights keep the original. This removes the BW24_RP=0 requirement — W4A8 MMQ prefill now coexists with the A6 split-plane decode wins (one resident copy serves both). DEFAULT-FLIP: vendored MMQ prefill (NVFP4 W4A8 + Q4_K/Q5_K int8-MMA, the exact pair the predecessor's BW24_MMQ_W4A8=1 arm engaged) is now DEFAULT-ON; BW24_MMQ_W4A8=0 = escape hatch to the int8 GEMM; BW24_MMQ=1 still opts GGUF-layout NVFP4 into W4A4.
kernel: load_tiles_nvfp4_w4a8<mmq_y, need_check, is_rp> in cu/mmq_nvfp4_w4a8.cu; C-ABI bw24_mmq_nvfp4_w4a8 gains rp arg; new kernel_check gate MMQ-W4A8-RP (bit-identity rp vs GGUF loader)

label: positive
notes: The rung the predecessor left: W4A8 MMQ no longer forfeits the A6 decode wins. Arm (b) W4A8+rp == arm (c) prime within 1.6% while holding arm (a) decode exactly — the coexistence goal met. DEFAULT-FLIP taken: mmq_w4a8_enabled() (BW24_MMQ_W4A8 != 0) now gates the whole vendored MMQ prefill suite in mmq_supports; lib.rs matmul/matmul_pre MMQ gates collapsed to mmq_supports (policy in one place). Full battery re-run green on the flipped default. env law unchanged (BW24_FAST/GEMM/MMVQ/FA_VEC); BW24_MMQ_W4A8 dropped from the required env — it is the default.

change: ARC B chunk-prime dequant-once: fa_prefill_q's inline dequant re-read+re-dequanted the whole quantized KV stream from every (q-block CTA x n_head) at chunk prime (64x+ redundant at chunk=4096; fa_prefill_q = 30.5%/153ms-launch of the 32k prime wall on the 40k trace). Fix in 3 stacked rungs, all bit-identical: (1) fa_dequant_kv_ws_bf16 dequants [t_kv, kv_dim] K(q8_0)+V(q5_1) ONCE per (layer,chunk) into a resident bf16 workspace (Engine.prime_deqw_ws, grown-and-reused across layers/chunks, ~83MB at 40k 27B) storing exactly __float2bfloat16(dq_*_elem(...)) = the value fa_prefill_q staged to smem; fa_prefill_qw is the byte-identical MMA/softmax/PV twin reading the workspace -> bit-identical O. 32k prime 26.41->18.11s. (2) vectorized 16B uint4 workspace->smem staging (pure byte copy, same smem bytes): 18.11->17.10s. (3) fa_prefill_qw_db cp.async double-buffered staging twin (+32KB smem second K/V tile pair, 1 CTA/SM vs 2, launch_bounds 128,1): prefetch tile n+1 during tile n MMA, cp.async.wait_group 1 pipeline; 17.10->16.51s. DEFAULT ON via BW24_PRIME_DEQW (=0 -> inline-dequant fa_prefill_q) and BW24_PRIME_DEQW_DB (=0 -> single-buffer twin).
kernel: fa_dequant_kv_ws_bf16 (grid-stride 1thr/elem) + fa_prefill_qw / fa_prefill_qw_db (BLOCK_Q=64 BK=32 4-warp, same tile/loop structure as fa_prefill_q)

measure.protocol: 27B NVFP4, BW24_GEN_ONLY prime timer, BW24_PRIME_CHUNK=4096, N=3 (spread <0.05s)
measure.nsys_32k: fa_prefill_q inline 30.5%/153ms-launch (pre) -> fa_prefill_qw_db 24.3%/26.6ms avg (144 launches incl 16 small); mul_mat_q_nvfp4_w4a8 now 52.5% = the next prime wall
measure.ncu_16k_qw_single: mem 66% / SM 15% / DRAM 0.6%, occupancy 16.4%, 255 regs, 2-CTA smem-limited -> staging-stalled, motivated the db twin

gates: kernel-check ALL GREEN incl new fa_prefill_view_ws-vs-inline bitdiff=0 x3 shapes (both twins); run-gen argmax 27B 1178 MATCH, 9B 268 MATCH; run-spec K=1..8 PASS both models; p3 chunk=4096 token-md5 IDENTICAL across deqw-on(db)/deqw-off/monolithic; 16k+32k deep prompts SELF-CONSISTENCY PASS
lesson: redundant-work kernels: move the shared decode out of the redundant grid dimension into a once-per-call workspace pass; keep the consumer a byte-identical twin (same staged values, same FP order) so exactness gates stay bitdiff=0. cp.async db on top pays even at 1 CTA/SM when the single-buffer kernel is staging-stalled (mem 66%/compute 15%).

change: ARC A candidate 2 (32B-ALIGNED SPLIT-PLANE K LAYOUT) — MEASURED NEGATIVE at the microbench gate, NO engine wiring. Theory under test: K q8_0 34B/block interleaved (2B scale + 32B qs) makes every qs read straddle a 32B transaction boundary; split-plane (qs plane [max_ctx][nblk][32B] perfectly aligned + separate 2B scale plane, mirror of the A6 weight repack) should cut sector traffic ~2x at DRAM depths. Probe: standalone probe/kv_align_probe.cu — verbatim copies of fa_decode_vec_q (register) and fa_decode_vec_q_smem (deep-ctx) with ONLY the K addressing remapped in the _al twins, 27B geometry (nh=24 nhkv=4 hd=256, grid/block/split-ladder mirror of the engine launcher), 8 rotating layer buffers so L2 behaves like the real multi-layer walk, partials verified BIT-IDENTICAL between layouts before timing (pure address remap, dequant math unchanged).
kernel: probe-only: fa_decode_vec_q_al / fa_decode_vec_q_smem_al twins in probe/kv_align_probe.cu (never wired into the engine)
seam: none — decision gate (aligned must beat interleaved >5% at 32k+) failed, so the BW24_KV_ALIGN seam was never built

measure.protocol: clock-locked 1860, N=5 runs x 100 iters, 8-layer rotation, exactness bitdiff=0 all cells

gates: probe-internal exactness gate: partO/partM/partL bitdiff=0 aligned-vs-interleaved at 8k/16k/32k/64k on both paths (dequant math unchanged, address remap only). Full engine battery NOT run — nothing wired.
lesson: TRANSACTION-STRADDLE THEORY DIES ON THE MEASUREMENT: the aligned layout DOES cut L1 sector traffic exactly as theorized (1.22->1.07 sectors/request, -11% sectors) but the kernel duration is IDENTICAL (256.06 vs 256.03us at 32k) — fa_decode at deep ctx is LATENCY-bound (DRAM 15% of peak, warps 34%), not transaction-bound, so saved sectors buy zero time. Sector metrics alone cannot justify a layout change; the roofline position decides. Side observations: (a) reg-aligned wins +4.5% at 8k only (the one cell where reg is transaction-sensitive) — below the wire bar and 8k decode is not the blocker; (b) in this 8-layer-rotating synthetic the smem twin beats the register path 2.1-2.3x at EVERY depth incl 8k (68.7 vs 159.8us) — the engine's BW24_FA_SMEM_TKV=16384 crossover may be re-sweepable on the real trace (real decode has other kernels evicting L2 between FA calls, so the synthetic overstates it), possible cheap follow-up; (c) remaining ARC A room is NOT in KV addressing — the +18ms/tok decay vs 0.76ms wall-math must live in latency/occupancy structure (combine chain, n_splits tail, launch overhead) not byte traffic.

kernel: qmatvec_{nvfp4,q8_0,q4_K,q5_K,q6_K}_mmvq_b8 (+_pf/_r2/_r2w8/_rp/_rpr2/_rpr2w8 NVFP4, _r2 k-quants)
change: 27B K=4 spec-decode cliff fix: mcols=8 batched weight-resident matvec tier. ROOT CAUSE CORRECTION vs the HANDOVER theory: T=5..8 verify did NOT split b4+b1 — matmul_decode_exact/matmul_pre gated batched at m<=4, so T=5 fell all the way to per-row grid.y=m MMVQ (qmatvec_nvfp4_mmvq_rp, 6448 launches @49us in the K=4 nsys, m full weight reads/launch = 5x weight traffic). Fix: b8 template instantiations of the EXISTING batched bodies (columns c>=m masked, dp4a order per (token,row) verbatim), dispatch extended to 2..=8 in matmul/matmul_pre/matmul_decode_exact, mcols map 2/4/8. b8 AUTO variant = r2w8/rpr2w8 everywhere (DRAM-cold rp msweep m=5/6/8 x 5 27B shapes: w8 wins/ties all cells except ffn_gate m=5/6 where rpr2 edges 1-3%; e2e K=4 p3: rpr2w8 97.9 vs b4-wave-rule auto 93.3 vs rpr2 91.4 tok/s — the b4 wave thresholds mispick rp for b8 register classes). k-quant b8 base+r2 twins added same recipe (q4_K absent from the K=4 profile; q5_K draft lm_head present -> covered).

measure.protocol: 27B p3 (6257 tok) FRSPEC_TRIM pmin0.15 NGEN=256, clock-locked 1860, N=3 (spread <=0.2%)
measure.acceptance: K3 67.3% / K4 55.2% / K5 49.5% / K6 44.1% — acceptance NOT the cliff (held across fix)
measure.verdict: K=3 remains the 27B optimum at pmin0.15 (acceptance decay outruns the kernel win); K=4..6 now degrade smoothly (-8.6%/-10.9%/-14.6% vs K=3) instead of cliffing

seam: BW24_B8=0 (keeps m=5..8 on per-row MMVQ; m=2..4 batched untouched), BW24_NO_BATCHED still global, BW24_MMVQ_BV forces b8 variants too
gates: kernel-check 126 OK 0 FAIL incl new b8 cells m=5/6/8 (BATCHED rel=0.00e0 NVFP4+all k-quants, RP BATCHED bit-bad=0 both shapes); run-gen argmax 1178/268 MATCH; run-spec K=1..8 self-consistency PASS both models; 27B p3 real-prompt PASS K=3..6 all N=3 runs
lesson: (1) 'b4+b1 split' was a plausible-but-wrong theory — the dispatch ceiling made T=5 fall to the WORST path (per-row, m weight reads), not a two-launch split; read the dispatch before trusting a remembered mechanism. (2) variant auto-rules do not transfer across register classes: the b4 wave-crossing rule picks rp for b8 shapes where rpr2w8 wins 25% — re-measure per MCOLS tier.

change: MoE async-prefetch arc (SLRU sync-DMA-on-miss open item): miss-staging profile says NOT WORTH IT — arc closed without building it
kernel: none (measurement verdict)

result.miss_staging_fraction: nsys decode window (128 tok, 15.19 ms/tok under profiler = 1944 ms): ALL H2D memcpy = 70.5 ms / 6511 copies / 2.97 GB = 3.6% of decode wall (<5% bar). Host-side cuMemcpyHtoDAsync issue cost only 17 ms. And this is the WARMUP-heavy window: first quarter carries 1.60 GB of the 2.97 GB; last quarter only 278 MB (6.6 ms, 1.4% of wall)
result.hit_rate_trajectory: steady-state window after 16-tok warmup: 90.8% @64 tok (41.8 MB/tok), 93.6% @128 (29.1 MB/tok), 96.3% @512 (16.6 MB/tok) — misses self-extinguish as the hot set goes resident on 96GB; decode tok/s RISES with window (74.9 tg128 -> 83.4 tg1024) for the same reason
result.decode_wall_anatomy_nsys: host API inside decode window: cuLaunchKernel 573 ms/185k calls, cuMemcpyDtoHAsync 306 ms/5.2k calls (router logits DtoH ~41/tok), memset+alloc+free ~370 ms — the wall is launch/bookkeeping + kernel time, NOT PCIe staging
result.fused_router_ab: BW24_FUSED_ROUTER=1 N=3: 73.3/73.4/73.4 vs 74.9 default — 2% WORSE at t=1, stays off

label: negative
notes: NEGATIVE-NOT-WORTH-IT: perfect one-layer-ahead copy_stream prefetch overlap caps the win at <=3.6% of wall and the 3.6% is warmup-concentrated (steady state ~1.4%); the risk side is the copy_stream hazard set (store-before-evict barrier + NO_EVT default) for a vanishing return. Reference on same box same run-day: llama.cpp b9863 35B-A3B IQ4_XS tg128 235.4+-2.7, pp512 8527+-36 (bw24 decode 0.32x llama — the gap is kernel+launch structure, not PCIe).

change: ARC 2 (gemma4-26b-a4b MoE): loader cannot take it — EXACT port-gap list recorded (the MiniMax-plan-style deliverable) + llama.cpp reference numbers for the eventual vs-dense comparison
kernel: none

result.model: gemma4-26b-a4b-q4km.gguf (15.63 GiB, arch=gemma4, 30 layers, 128 experts x 8 used, expert_ff 704, n_embd 2816, vocab 262144)
result.load_attempt: HybridModel::load panics not-a-hybrid-arch (hybrid.rs:262); load_dense would fail too — ModelConfig parses arch as Other(gemma4), moe: None, per-layer arrays ignored
port_gap_list: 1. FUSED ffn_gate_up_exps [2816,1408,128] Q4_K single tensor (no separate gate/up) => loader split or fused gate-up kernel path

2. scale sidecars: ffn_down_exps.scale F32[128] per-expert + ffn_gate_inp.scale F32[2816] router-input scale (llama.cpp: router logits = mm(gate_inp, rms_norm(attn_out) * 1/sqrt(n_embd) * gate_inp_s)) => new dequant epilogue + router prologue

3. per-layer attention shapes: head_count_kv ARRAY [8,8,8,8,8,2,...] + sliding_window_pattern [1,1,1,1,1,0,...] — 25 SWA layers (window 1024, head_dim 256, rope base 10k) interleaved with 5 global layers (blk 5,11,17,23,29: head_dim 512, kv_heads 2, rope base 1M with rope_freqs freq_factors); dual key_length 512/key_length_swa 256 => per-layer attention dispatch + SWA mask, none of which bw24 has
4. V-LESS global layers: blk.{5,11,17,23,29} have NO attn_v.weight — llama.cpp gemma4.cpp:247 uses Vcur = Kcur (V IS the K projection) + rms_norm on V => new attention wiring

5. block wiring: parallel shared-MLP + MoE branches BOTH from attn_out, summed (cur_mlp + cur_moe); dual post-norms (post_ffw_norm_1/2, pre_ffw_norm_2) + post_attention_norm + layer_output_scale[1] multiplier + input embd scale sqrt(n_embd)
6. final_logit_softcapping 30.0 (scale, tanh, scale lm_head epilogue) — no softcap anywhere in bw24
7. GELU activation for shared FFN AND experts (LLM_FFN_GELU) — bw24 kernels only have silu
8. Q5_0 dequant MISSING: blk.3.ffn_down{,_exps} are Q5_0; bw24-gguf dequantize() panics (type in enum, no path) and no qmatvec/mmvq kernel
9. tokenizer.ggml.model = gemma4 (sentencepiece-scored) — bw24-tokenizer hard-errors on anything but gpt2 BPE
result.vs_dense_context: bw24 27B dense NVFP4 decode 67.9 tok/s (w4a8-rp JSONL row) vs llama gemma4-MoE tg128 224 — the gemma4 MoE-vs-dense comparison on bw24 itself is blocked on the port above

label: neutral
notes: Extends the 2026-07-04 STAGE-4 6-item scope with 3 new hard gaps found by full metadata+tensor audit: Q5_0 dequant, gemma4 tokenizer, V-less global layers (V=K trick). Port is bounded but real: loader arm + 4 kernel epilogues (gelu, softcap, router-scale, per-expert-scale) + SWA/per-layer-KV attention dispatch + Q5_0 + tokenizer. Alternative validated in STAGE 4 stays: pick a qwen3moe/olmoe-shaped second MoE for zero-port shape breadth.

change: LAUNCH-STRUCTURE STAGES 1+2: single-sync pinned fused-router readback DEFAULT-ON (router DtoH elision) + MoE decode memset/alloc churn elision
kernel: moe_router_topk_f32 (unchanged kernel, new host readback protocol) + alloc-policy changes only

baseline.note: 0fff9706 main, env law + BW24_MOE_CACHE=1, free clocks

result.stage1_router_dtoh: root cause of the old 2%-WORSE fused-router verdict FOUND: the =1 arm paid TWO full stream syncs (dtoh_i32(sel) then dtoh(w), each clone_dtoh+synchronize) + 2 alloc_zeros memsets per MoE layer, vs ONE sync on the host route's single logits dtoh. Fix = moe_router_topk_host: uninit outputs, both DtoH issued async into a persistent PINNED (flags=0 cacheable, NOT the write-combined cudarc default) staging buffer, ONE synchronize. 64B/token readback replaces 1KB logits + host softmax-256 + stable sort. DEFAULT ON all t (BW24_FUSED_ROUTER=0 rollback) so decode and spec-verify route identically (FP-order lesson 8 class)
result.stage2_memset_alloc: moe_out alloc_zeros->uninit when gdec can fire (moe_down8_fma fully overwrites its row; sequential-fallback tokens lazily memset only their row); per-layer scratch_g/u/d trio (3x ~1MB alloc_zeros+memset+free per MoE layer per token, DEAD code under BW24_MOE_CACHE) now lazy+uninit; act/sa/g zeros->uninit (silu_mul/sigmoid full-overwrite); router sel/w alloc_zeros->uninit
result.nsys_delta: memset 149.5k->60.6k calls; cuMemcpyDtoHAsync 306ms/20.7k->134ms/20.7k (issue cost, sync moved); alloc/free pairs 587k->576k

Design pick was option (c) indirect-kernel from the arc brief — no graph needed, kills the router round-trip AND rides the existing gdec 2-launch shape.

change: CUDA-graph replay for the 35B MoE decode step RE-MEASURED after stage 3 — NEGATIVE, do not enable
kernel: none (measurement verdict; generate_graph already runs MoE through decode_step_dc_cap)

result.mechanism: the dev path made MoE launches cheap (2/layer) and killed the DtoH stalls, so the graph's launch-batching savings shrank below its costs: 3x capture warmup runs per t_kv bucket + the capture-time router top-k gets BAKED (sel/w device buffers are re-routed every replay but the captured moe_ffn_dev launches carry capture-time sel POINTERS — correct because sel_d is re-written in-graph by the router kernel, but the extra fixed-shape overhead plus recapture-every-64-tokens churn nets negative)

clock_note: free clocks, decode-bench both mode (eager and graph same process, interleaved by construction)

accuracy.argmax: graph path produces MATCH (decode_bench gate) — correctness fine, perf negative

label: negative
notes: Same verdict class as graph-spec stage-3 remeasure: once launches are structurally removed, graphs have nothing left to reclaim and their capture/recapture overhead dominates. Do not re-open unless the decode becomes launch-bound again. The remaining 35B decode wall is now KERNEL time: 64k qmatvec_q8_0_mmvq m=1 launches for the 40 dense trunk projections (attn/norm class) = the next lever, plus fa_decode_vec_q 135us/call.
